recs.s: NEW & ACTIONABLE: Linter Miner (run 26690626184) failed with CAPIError 429 'Maximum effective tokens exceeded (25.13M/25M)' after exhausting 4 --continue retries (each just re-hits the cap, wasting ~5min). Two-part fix: (1) Reduce Linter Miner's effective-token footprint — chunk its linter-aggregation scope, trim per-file context, or split into multiple smaller runs so a single run stays under 25M eff_tok; (2) Harness change: detect the 429 'Maximum effective tokens exceeded' signature and FAIL FAST instead of retrying with --continue, since continuation cannot recover a hard budget cap. Ties into open #35661 (token-budget). This is the single clearest actionable failure today.

NEW & ACTIONABLE: PR Sous Chef (26690499578) failed pre-agent because 'make install-golangci-lint' piped a non-gzip golangci-lint v2.12.2 release body into tar (exit 2) in the shared 'Install development dependencies' step, killing the agent at 0 turns. Harden the install-golangci-lint Makefile target (~Makefile:403): download to a temp file, verify HTTP 200 + gzip magic bytes before extracting, retry with backoff (3 attempts), pin and verify a checksum, and prefer the Actions cache / a vendored binary over the live release endpoint each run. Shared step => fixes flake for all dependent workflows. The Failure Investigator already filed a durable tracking issue for this.

REACTIVATED & ACTIONABLE: Contribution Check (26689948645) safe_outputs step failed because one add_comment had target=* with no number on a schedule event, hard-failing the whole job and discarding a successful create_issue. Fix (two parts): (1) Make 'Process Safe Outputs' treat an individual invalid item as skipped-with-warning when >=1 item succeeded, so one malformed add_comment cannot nullify a good create_issue; (2) Validate target=* at the MCP emit boundary — reject an add_comment whose context cannot resolve to a concrete issue/PR number, returning a tool error so the agent self-corrects in-loop rather than failing post-run. Separately investigate why create_issue with temporary_id 'aw_summary' registered 0 temp ids (broke add_labels chaining). Prompt-only guardrails proved insufficient (the model violated an explicit instruction).

NEW: The [aw] Failure Investigator (6h) (26692427111) determined 'zero failures in the 6h window' early (~19:11) but ran the full 60 minutes / 7.8M tokens / $7.15 — the most expensive run of the day — and red-failed on the 60-min Claude CLI timeout. Investigate why it does not early-exit/converge on a clean window (it should emit a short noop/'no failures' report and stop). Confirm whether the 60-min step ceiling is intended; if a clean window legitimately needs <5min, the hour-long burn is wasted cost. Smoke Claude's 10-min timeout (26690105542) is a separate PR-branch smoke artifact on the merged gateway-bump PR #35973 — monitor the MCP 'client is closing: EOF' cluster for recurrence on main, no fix filed.

CARRIED: Avenger absent two consecutive windows (05-29, 05-30); --max-turns 25 / isMaxTurnsExit issue still unverified. Keep the 'set max-turns: ~50 in .github/workflows/avenger.md' recommendation open; re-evaluate on the next window in which Avenger runs.

CARRIED: serena find_symbol/get_symbols_overview return 'MCP error 0 EOF' on first query (Go LSP backend crash after activate_project). Capture serena/gopls stderr, check gopls/Go-toolchain compatibility, and confirm whether a warm-up/index step is needed before symbol queries. Re-check on a clean Smoke Claude run (today's Smoke Claude failed on an unrelated CLI timeout).
WATCH: cost ticked back up to $5.24 (24.5M eff_tok) from $3.07, still below the $11.33 peak. No action; monitor for re-escalation of the prompt-growth pattern toward the prior high.
